ogtitle: Driving start-up innovation with Lighter Capital
content: Skip to login   Skip to main content   Skip to chat     Business     Small business     Business Moments     Growing your business     Driving start-up innovation with Lighter Capital   Driving start-up innovation with Lighter Capital   We’re here to drive innovation and support start-ups.    Our collaboration with Lighter Capital    Getting a tech start-up off the ground is equal parts challenge and exhilaration. Juggling the drive to deliver your vision, while searching for the right long-term financial partners can be tough. But it’s crucial to get this balance right in order to hit your growth milestones. We’ve recognised a market gap for tech start-ups ready for funding but not traditional bank debt. Filling this gap is what inspired our collaboration with Lighter Capital.    Meet Lighter Capital    Lighter Capital is a leading fintech lender that supports early stage tech-enabled (SaaS-based) companies. Founded in 2010 in the U.S. it now has a presence in both Canada and Australia – providing up to $4.25m revenue-based financing to meet the demanding working capital needs of this market.    A head start for your start-up    The collaboration between NAB and Lighter Capital is good news for early stage start-ups. It means:     access to potential funding solutions from Lighter Capital  access to a dedicated NAB small business specialist if you choose to open a NAB business transaction account  a seamless transition from Lighter Capital to NAB if you choose to bank with us down the track, as you grow  access to industry insights, resources and networking events.    Is my business eligible?    Lighter Capital’s products are designed to fill a specific gap in the market. You could be eligible if your business:     is a tech-based start-up (e.g. Software, SaaS, tech services)  has at least $20,000 monthly recurring revenue  has been operational for at least 12 months, with at least 6 months of recurring revenue  is based and incorporated in Australia.    Get started    Discover how Lighter Capital could help your start-up grow.    Learn more about Lighter Capital , opens in new window   Get in touch   Contact us   Explore our business banking contact information and get support with a wide range of products, services and topics.    View all business banking contacts   Visit a NAB branch   Our business bankers are located all around Australia.    Find a NAB business banking centre   Important information    This section contains Important Information relevant to the page you are viewing, but you can't see it because you have JavaScript disabled on your browser. Please enable JavaScript and come back so you can see the complete page. It's important that you read the Important Information in this section before acting on any information on this page.    Apologies but the Important Information section you are trying to view is not displaying properly at the moment. Please refresh the page or try again later.   NAB Ventures, the venture capital investment arm of the National Australia Bank Group (NAB Group), is a minority shareholder of Lighter Capital.    Lighter Capital remains a separate legal entity and is not a part of the NAB Group. The services and products offered by Lighter Capital are solely issued by Lighter Capital and do not represent deposits or other liabilities of the NAB Group. National Australia Bank Limited ABN 12 004 044 937 AFSL and Australian Credit Licence 230686 (NAB) isn’t involved in the credit writing or decision making for Lighter Capital products.    NAB does not stand behind, guarantee or support the financial obligations of Lighter Capital.    There’s no commission paid between NAB and Lighter Capital.    The information contained in this article is intended to be of a general nature only. It has been prepared without taking into account any person’s objectives, financial situation or needs. Before acting on this information, NAB recommends that you consider whether it is appropriate for your circumstances. NAB recommends that you seek independent legal, financial and taxation advice before acting on any information in this article.
